title: Search Head Cluster Status (captain election storms, KV store lag, conf-replication backlog, deployer desync, member CPU failover risk)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.1.1 indexer queue saturation analytics, UC-13.1.3 forwarder connectivity narratives, UC-13.1.11 indexer cluster replication health, or per-index storage fullness cards in isolation; does not automatically mutate server.conf or elect captains; produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved recovery actions.
evidence: Saved search platform_uc_13_1_10_shc_cluster_status with five-minute schedule, Monitoring Console Search Head Clustering and KV Store drilldowns, weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted platform_evidence index, and shc_inventory.csv version hash stored with change tickets.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab search head cluster, induce repeated captain migration or ConfReplicationThread backlog language while metrics show multiple offline peers, confirm platform_uc_13_1_10_shc_cluster_status returns critical or high within ten minutes with non-null recommended_action and deployer_push_status context.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring stable captain, converged bundles, and healthy KV primaries with maintenance metadata recorded, confirm severity returns to low or medium within two evaluation windows and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

dataSources: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with components SHCRaftConsensus, SHCStateMachine, ConfReplicationThread for RAFT consensus and conf-replication backlog language; source=*metrics.log group=shclustering for peer heartbeats, captain identity, and member posture; source=*mongod.log* and component=MongoCloudWatcher for KV store primary elections and oplog lag hints; component=SHCRESTAPI and action=apply_shcluster_bundle for deployer push outcomes; source=*introspection* with data.search_props.runtime for per-member CPU pressure; lookup shc_inventory.csv enriches env, owner_team, and deployment_role by cluster_id; optional join to metrics.log group=server for build_version parity per member_host.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.1.10 SHC health: RAFT consensus churn, conf-replication backlog signals, KV Store oplog lag, deployer bundle apply posture, member CPU saturation from introspection. Tunables earliest=-4h@h cap_page=2 conf_page=500 kv_page_ms=5000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=SHCRaftConsensus OR component=SHCStateMachine OR component=ConfReplicationThread)
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval captain_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(captain, captain_uri, cluster_captain, ""))))
      | eval lane="consensus"
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval election_evt=if(match(lm,"captain|election|raft|vote|leader|step down|standby|consensus|renegotiate"),1,0)
      | eval conf_replication_backlog=if(match(lm,"conf-replication|conf replication|configuration replication|repl backlog"), 50, 0)
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)backlog\D{0,12}(?<cback>\d{1,7})"
      | eval conf_replication_backlog=coalesce(tonumber(cback,10), conf_replication_backlog)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host captain_id lane election_evt conf_replication_backlog ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=shclustering earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval captain_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(captain, captain_uri, ""))))
      | eval lane="shc_metrics"
      | eval peer_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status, peer_status, peerStatus, ""))))
      | eval member_offline=if(match(coalesce(peer_status,"up"),"up|online|started|active"),0,1)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host captain_id lane member_offline ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (source=*mongod.log* OR component=MongoCloudWatcher)
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="kv_mongo"
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)(?:oplog|replication|catchup)\D{0,36}(?<lagms>\d{1,9})\s*ms"
      | eval kv_store_oplog_lag_ms=coalesce(tonumber(lagms,10), if(match(lm,"election|primary|secondary|catchup|rollback"), 1200, 0))
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host lane kv_store_oplog_lag_ms ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=SHCRESTAPI OR action=apply_shcluster_bundle)
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="deployer"
      | eval lm=lower(toString(coalesce(action, log_level, _raw)))
      | eval deployer_push_status=if(match(lm,"error|fail|timeout|denied|rollback|invalid|mismatch|abort|cannot|503|eai|unauthorized"),1,0)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host lane deployer_push_status ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*introspection* earliest=-4h@h latest=now data.search_props.runtime=*
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="intro_cpu"
      | eval cpu_pct=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(cpu_system_pct, cpu_user_pct, cpu_idle_pct, 0))))
      | eval cpu_pct=if(cpu_pct<=0 AND isnotnull(cpu_idle_pct), 100-tonumber(cpu_idle_pct,10), cpu_pct)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host lane cpu_pct ]
| eval cluster_id=coalesce(nullif(cluster_id,""),"unknown_cluster")
| eval member_host=coalesce(member_host,"unknown_member")
| eval captain_id=coalesce(captain_id,"unset_captain")
| fillnull value=0 election_evt conf_replication_backlog member_offline kv_store_oplog_lag_ms deployer_push_status cpu_pct
| sort 0 cluster_id - _time
| streamstats window=18 current=t global=f sum(election_evt) AS election_stream_tail BY cluster_id
| eventstats dc(member_host) AS member_count BY cluster_id
| bin span=5m _time
| stats sum(election_evt) AS captain_changes_5m
        max(election_stream_tail) AS election_stream_peak
        max(conf_replication_backlog) AS conf_replication_backlog
        max(kv_store_oplog_lag_ms) AS kv_store_oplog_lag_ms
        max(deployer_push_status) AS deployer_push_max
        sum(member_offline) AS member_offline_count
        max(member_count) AS member_count
        latest(member_host) AS member_host
        latest(captain_id) AS captain_id
        dc(captain_id) AS captain_dc
        max(cpu_pct) AS member_peak_cpu
    BY cluster_id _time
| sort 0 cluster_id - _time
| dedup cluster_id
| eval captain_changes_5m=coalesce(captain_changes_5m, election_stream_peak, 0)
| eval deployer_push_status=if(coalesce(deployer_push_max,0)>0,"out_of_sync","ok")
| eval member_p95_cpu_pct=coalesce(member_peak_cpu,0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(captain_changes_5m,0)>=2 OR coalesce(conf_replication_backlog,0)>500 OR coalesce(kv_store_oplog_lag_ms,0)>5000, "critical",
    coalesce(member_offline_count,0)>=2 OR deployer_push_status=="out_of_sync" OR coalesce(captain_dc,0)>=3, "high",
    coalesce(member_p95_cpu_pct,0)>=85 OR coalesce(member_offline_count,0)==1, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "stabilize_captain_quorum_review_raft_network_kv_primary_and_conf_replication_broker",
    severity=="high", "reconcile_deployer_bundle_paths_resync_members_validate_build_parity",
    severity=="medium", "scale_search_capacity_or_rolling_restart_hot_member_after_dispatch_audit",
    true(), "monitor_continue_mc_shc_and_kv_store_dashboards")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup shc_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(cluster_id)))
     | dedup cluster_id
     | fields cluster_id env owner_team deployment_role ]
| eval env=coalesce(env,"unknown")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eventstats median(member_count) AS member_count_baseline by env
| join type=left max=0 member_host
    [| search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=server
     | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, member_host))))
     | dedup member_host
     | table member_host build_version ]
| table _time cluster_id env owner_team captain_id captain_changes_5m member_count member_offline_count conf_replication_backlog kv_store_oplog_lag_ms deployer_push_status member_p95_cpu_pct severity recommended_action
description: Detects search head cluster health degradation by correlating RAFT consensus and ConfReplicationThread backlog signals, shclustering metrics for member offline posture, KV store mongod and MongoCloudWatcher lag and election language, deployer REST and apply_shcluster_bundle failure hints, and introspection CPU saturation proxies, then enriching fleet metadata and build versions for routing.
value: Platform leaders page before users see captain unavailable errors by fusing consensus churn, configuration replication backlog, KV primary instability, deployer drift, and member resource pressure into one analyst-ready row per cluster with explicit recommended actions tied to operational runbooks.
implementation: Schedule platform_uc_13_1_10_shc_cluster_status every five minutes with earliest=-4h@h from the Monitoring Console or platform search head, maintain shc_inventory.csv for env and ownership, tune captain and backlog thresholds per environment class, route critical rows to platform search on-call with MC SHC and KV drilldowns, and archive weekly alert exports for governance evidence.
visualization: Severity-colored table by cluster_id with drilldowns to consensus components, shclustering metrics slices, mongod lag lines, deployer REST errors, introspection CPU trends, MC Search Head Clustering and KV Store: Cluster Status embeds, and single-value tiles for captain_changes_5m and kv_store_oplog_lag_ms.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries when introspection-derived CPU metrics map to Performance hosts; accelerate Inventory summaries for cluster and member asset objects maintained in shc_inventory.csv; accelerate Change summaries when deployer and bundle actions are modeled for correlation; retain fourteen days hot for incident replay.

references.title: Splunk Docs — About search head clustering
Splunk Docs — KV store high availability
Splunk Docs — Update common peer configurations and apps
Splunk Docs — Use the monitoring console
Splunk Docs — Distributed Splunk Enterprise overview

knownFalsePositives: Captain handover during a planned upgrade or dynamic captain rebuild can look like an election storm until you correlate change_ticket_id metadata and maintenance banners; require dwell and inventory tags before paging application owners. Deployer push exercises on a canary cluster intentionally generate REST errors in lower environments; gate non-production clusters with env labels so synthetic failures never wake production on-call. Blue-green style migrations across stacks can elevate captain churn and KV catch-up lag for hours while operations validate parity; treat elevated medium severity as expected until the migration runbook declares cutover complete. Planned member maintenance removes peers from service by design; member_offline_count equals one in those windows is often benign when the change calendar shows a rolling restart and remaining members stay healthy. RBAC user-load tests and executive drill sessions can spike CPU on members without implying consensus failure; pair member_p95_cpu_pct with concurrent search counts from introspection before declaring cluster instability. Cold replica catch-up after backup restores or storage remediation inflates kv_store_oplog_lag_ms without data loss; validate lag decay slopes and disk latency rather than opening a sev-1 on the first high sample. Brief conf-replication spikes during large knowledge object deployments can exceed quiet baselines until peers converge; compare ConfReplicationThread density against deployer completion before muting. Monitoring Console service restarts can create short gaps in distilled tiles while raw _internal remains complete; confirm Job Inspector scan coverage before trusting an empty MC tile as truth. Certificate transparency or vulnerability scanning tools that open TLS probes against peer ports may log alarming REST narratives without user impact; correlate scanner schedules with SHCRESTAPI noise. Search affinity misconfiguration can run this saved search on a host that does not forward all member logs, producing false calm; validate distributed search peer list and index access roles quarterly.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the senior engineers who operate search head clusters, deployer hosts, monitoring console search heads, and the configuration management pipelines that publish apps to those fleets. This use case targets the interactive search delivery plane inside Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud dedicated stacks: a search head cluster is a set of peers that elect a captain through a RAFT-style consensus layer, replicate knowledge bundles through a configuration replication broker, maintain a highly available embedded database for key-value collections, and accept deployer-driven bundle pushes that must converge identically on every peer before users feel pain. The captain is not a second scheduler for ingestion; it coordinates cluster metadata, artifact replication health, and certain administrative transitions. Members can be dynamically added or removed, and dynamic captain rebuild events are normal in small doses but pathological when repeated under disk pressure or network partitions. The KV store primary mirrors operational state for collections; when primaries flap or oplog lag stretches, dashboards that depend on collections and certain modular inputs degrade before classic search logs scream. Conf replication backlog shows up as ConfReplicationThread language and related metrics; when it grows without bound, peers drift invisibly until searches fail in confusing ways. Deployer pushes that collide on bundle paths or skip validation surface as SHCRESTAPI errors or apply_shcluster_bundle failures, leaving some members on stale knowledge while others advance.

Differentiation keeps sibling gold cards honest. UC-13.1.1 remains authoritative for indexer-side parsing and index queue saturation after bytes already arrived on disk. UC-13.1.3 remains authoritative for universal forwarder and heavy forwarder tcpout health toward indexers. UC-13.1.11 remains authoritative for indexer cluster replication, CMReplicationManager fixup pressure, and bucket copy compliance. This UC answers whether the search head cluster can sustain a stable captain, converged bundles, healthy KV primaries, and enough CPU headroom on members to survive the next failover. Splunk Cloud operators should treat stack traces and internal identifiers as confidential; restrict roles that can export wide windows of index=_internal from production tenants.

Risk briefing: aggressive thresholds during approved rolling upgrades will still page unless you correlate change_ticket_id metadata. Certificate rotations that touch peer-to-peer listeners can masquerade as consensus instability until trust chains stabilize. NTP drift produces RAFT timeouts that look like malicious flapping. Large saved-search migration projects can spike CPU on the captain-adjacent members and trigger false failover pressure. OOM killer events on Linux hosts running splunkd can remove the captain abruptly; pair this UC with host memory analytics. ReplicationManager queues on artifact paths interact with dispatch directory growth; when dispatch grows without housekeeping, garbage collection pauses amplify tail latency on interactive searches.

RBAC and privacy: _internal and introspection logs expose hostnames, file paths, search names, and sometimes customer-adjacent strings embedded in poorly named knowledge objects. Route alert evidence to restricted indexes, watermark exports, and avoid mailing raw _raw lines to broad distribution lists.

Collector posture: forward splunkd logs from every search head cluster member and the deployer, ensure metrics.log includes group=shclustering on your build train, and preserve mongod-family logs for KV troubleshooting where your security policy allows. When Cloud operations restrict direct host access, rely on the same signals through the observability pipeline they expose to your tenant.

CLI baselines operators trust before opening sev-1 tickets include splunk show shcluster-status on a member with connectivity to all peers, splunk show kvstore-status when collections are in play, splunk btool check on deployer and member for accidental duplicate app paths, and openssl validation when mTLS between peers recently changed. Document expected maintenance windows in the inventory lookup so paging routes to the squad that owns the cluster label.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, consensus and configuration replication narratives: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with component in SHCRaftConsensus, SHCStateMachine, and ConfReplicationThread. These lines explain captain elections, state transitions, and conf-replication backlog language. Tune props.conf TRUNCATE high enough that long RAFT diagnostics remain intact during incidents. If your security team routes verbose INFO away, keep WARN and ERROR for these components on a hot path.

Lane B, shclustering metrics: index=_internal source=*metrics.log group=shclustering. Validate fields that describe peers, heartbeats, captain identity, and dynamic captain flags on your minor version. Align clocks across members; skewed time breaks any time-bucketed correlation you run adjacent to this UC.

Lane C, KV store operations: index=_internal with source matching mongod logs or component=MongoCloudWatcher in Splunk Cloud style deployments. Primary elections, rollback language, and oplog lag hints land here. If your organization forbids shipping raw mongod lines off-box, negotiate a redacted subset that still exposes lag milliseconds and role transitions.

Lane D, deployer and REST apply failures: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with component=SHCRESTAPI or action=apply_shcluster_bundle in structured audits. Ensure deployer host keys match the inventory lookup so joins do not collapse rows.

Lane E, member CPU saturation context: index=_internal source=*introspection* with data.search_props.runtime present. This lane approximates per-member CPU pressure that threatens timely heartbeat responses. Pair with Splunk_TA_nix for Linux run queue and memory pressure when you need second opinions outside splunkd introspection.

Supporting inventory: maintain shc_inventory.csv keyed on cluster_id with columns env, owner_team, and deployment_role at minimum. Refresh weekly during change season or automate KV store updates from configuration management. The cluster_id field in the CSV must match the normalized cluster_id produced by the coalesce ladder in the SPL; if your estate labels clusters by captain hostname today, migrate labels deliberately rather than muting the alert.

Monitoring Console posture: enable distributed mode, confirm search head clustering dashboards populate, and pin the Search Head Clustering view alongside KV Store: Cluster Status for operator triage. Use those dashboards to validate that peaks in this saved search align with visual spikes rather than collection gaps.

Deployer hygiene: standardize bundle staging directories, eliminate duplicate app folder names across staged paths, and document the supported workflow for splunk apply shcluster-bundle -answer-yes after rsync or robocopy completes. Never treat a partial copy as ready for production apply.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_10_shc_cluster_status with a five-minute schedule in production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after baselines settle. Throttle duplicate cluster_id rows for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager templates so on-call engineers open consensus logs, deployer transcripts, and MC drilldowns without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms—consensus and conf replication prose, shclustering metrics, KV mongod and watcher lanes, deployer REST and bundle apply lanes, and introspection CPU context—so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine cluster crisis. coalesce() ladders absorb field naming drift across minor versions for cluster_id, member_host, and captain_id. streamstats measures short-window election tail sums per cluster before five-minute bins collapse signals. eventstats establishes member_count per cluster before bins and a fleet median member_count baseline per env after the inventory join for contextual drift. case() maps captain churn, conf-replication backlog, KV oplog lag, offline members, deployer desync, captain identity churn, and CPU saturation into severity tiers with recommended_action routing tokens. join enriches rows from shc_inventory.csv for env and owner_team, then left joins build_version from metrics.log group=server for version skew triage.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.10 SHC health: RAFT consensus churn, conf-replication backlog signals, KV Store oplog lag, deployer bundle apply posture, member CPU saturation from introspection. Tunables earliest=-4h@h cap_page=2 conf_page=500 kv_page_ms=5000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=SHCRaftConsensus OR component=SHCStateMachine OR component=ConfReplicationThread)
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval captain_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(captain, captain_uri, cluster_captain, ""))))
      | eval lane="consensus"
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval election_evt=if(match(lm,"captain|election|raft|vote|leader|step down|standby|consensus|renegotiate"),1,0)
      | eval conf_replication_backlog=if(match(lm,"conf-replication|conf replication|configuration replication|repl backlog"), 50, 0)
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)backlog\D{0,12}(?<cback>\d{1,7})"
      | eval conf_replication_backlog=coalesce(tonumber(cback,10), conf_replication_backlog)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host captain_id lane election_evt conf_replication_backlog ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=shclustering earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval captain_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(captain, captain_uri, ""))))
      | eval lane="shc_metrics"
      | eval peer_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status, peer_status, peerStatus, ""))))
      | eval member_offline=if(match(coalesce(peer_status,"up"),"up|online|started|active"),0,1)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host captain_id lane member_offline ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (source=*mongod.log* OR component=MongoCloudWatcher)
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="kv_mongo"
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)(?:oplog|replication|catchup)\D{0,36}(?<lagms>\d{1,9})\s*ms"
      | eval kv_store_oplog_lag_ms=coalesce(tonumber(lagms,10), if(match(lm,"election|primary|secondary|catchup|rollback"), 1200, 0))
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host lane kv_store_oplog_lag_ms ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=SHCRESTAPI OR action=apply_shcluster_bundle)
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="deployer"
      | eval lm=lower(toString(coalesce(action, log_level, _raw)))
      | eval deployer_push_status=if(match(lm,"error|fail|timeout|denied|rollback|invalid|mismatch|abort|cannot|503|eai|unauthorized"),1,0)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host lane deployer_push_status ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*introspection* earliest=-4h@h latest=now data.search_props.runtime=*
      | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label, cluster_label, cluster_id, ""))))
      | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, ""))))
      | eval lane="intro_cpu"
      | eval cpu_pct=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(cpu_system_pct, cpu_user_pct, cpu_idle_pct, 0))))
      | eval cpu_pct=if(cpu_pct<=0 AND isnotnull(cpu_idle_pct), 100-tonumber(cpu_idle_pct,10), cpu_pct)
      | fields _time cluster_id member_host lane cpu_pct ]
| eval cluster_id=coalesce(nullif(cluster_id,""),"unknown_cluster")
| eval member_host=coalesce(member_host,"unknown_member")
| eval captain_id=coalesce(captain_id,"unset_captain")
| fillnull value=0 election_evt conf_replication_backlog member_offline kv_store_oplog_lag_ms deployer_push_status cpu_pct
| sort 0 cluster_id - _time
| streamstats window=18 current=t global=f sum(election_evt) AS election_stream_tail BY cluster_id
| eventstats dc(member_host) AS member_count BY cluster_id
| bin span=5m _time
| stats sum(election_evt) AS captain_changes_5m
        max(election_stream_tail) AS election_stream_peak
        max(conf_replication_backlog) AS conf_replication_backlog
        max(kv_store_oplog_lag_ms) AS kv_store_oplog_lag_ms
        max(deployer_push_status) AS deployer_push_max
        sum(member_offline) AS member_offline_count
        max(member_count) AS member_count
        latest(member_host) AS member_host
        latest(captain_id) AS captain_id
        dc(captain_id) AS captain_dc
        max(cpu_pct) AS member_peak_cpu
    BY cluster_id _time
| sort 0 cluster_id - _time
| dedup cluster_id
| eval captain_changes_5m=coalesce(captain_changes_5m, election_stream_peak, 0)
| eval deployer_push_status=if(coalesce(deployer_push_max,0)>0,"out_of_sync","ok")
| eval member_p95_cpu_pct=coalesce(member_peak_cpu,0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(captain_changes_5m,0)>=2 OR coalesce(conf_replication_backlog,0)>500 OR coalesce(kv_store_oplog_lag_ms,0)>5000, "critical",
    coalesce(member_offline_count,0)>=2 OR deployer_push_status=="out_of_sync" OR coalesce(captain_dc,0)>=3, "high",
    coalesce(member_p95_cpu_pct,0)>=85 OR coalesce(member_offline_count,0)==1, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "stabilize_captain_quorum_review_raft_network_kv_primary_and_conf_replication_broker",
    severity=="high", "reconcile_deployer_bundle_paths_resync_members_validate_build_parity",
    severity=="medium", "scale_search_capacity_or_rolling_restart_hot_member_after_dispatch_audit",
    true(), "monitor_continue_mc_shc_and_kv_store_dashboards")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup shc_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster_id=lower(trim(toString(cluster_id)))
     | dedup cluster_id
     | fields cluster_id env owner_team deployment_role ]
| eval env=coalesce(env,"unknown")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eventstats median(member_count) AS member_count_baseline by env
| join type=left max=0 member_host
    [| search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=server
     | eval member_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server, member_host))))
     | dedup member_host
     | table member_host build_version ]
| table _time cluster_id env owner_team captain_id captain_changes_5m member_count member_offline_count conf_replication_backlog kv_store_oplog_lag_ms deployer_push_status member_p95_cpu_pct severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_10_shc_cluster_status_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_10_shc_cluster_status | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance notes: if Job Inspector shows multisearch skew, materialize each lane hourly into a summary index keyed on cluster_id and lane, widen alert searches to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap subsearch fan-out on tiny search heads used for ad hoc work.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on a cluster member shell. Run splunk show shcluster-status and compare captain, stable members, and service counts to the latest row for the same cluster_id in the alert window. When Splunk disagrees with itself between REST and logs, verify that the search head running the saved search can read all peers and that SSL or firewall changes did not partition a site.

Deep dive conf replication: pick a row with conf_replication_backlog elevated, open contemporaneous ConfReplicationThread lines, and validate whether backlog language reflects a genuine broker stall or a benign burst during bundle activation. Pair with deployer timestamps to see whether a push coincides with the spike.

KV store validation: for kv_store_oplog_lag_ms spikes, read mongod or MongoCloudWatcher lines around the event, confirm whether a cold replica is catching up after maintenance, and verify disk latency on the member hosting the primary. Use MC KV Store: Cluster Status to confirm role transitions.

Deployer validation: replay apply_shcluster_bundle transcripts, confirm rsync or staging jobs completed cleanly, and verify no duplicate app names exist in the bundle path. When errors mention checksum mismatch, re-stage rather than forcing peers into divergent states.

Introspection cross-check: compare member_p95_cpu_pct proxies with Linux top or perf traces on the hottest member; confirm introspection sampling is not disabled by an overly aggressive server.conf tune.

Synthetic test: in lab, induce a controlled captain migration during a maintenance window with documented metadata and confirm severity returns to medium or low afterward without manual suppression. Negative test: on a calm cluster, confirm filtering severity to critical and high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds are temporarily raised in a clone.

btool validation: run splunk btool check --print on deployer and members after changes; unresolved duplicates should explain deployer_push_status out_of_sync narratives before you blame the network.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Split-brain after network partition: restore full mesh connectivity between members, validate firewall rules and MTU symmetry, rerun shcluster status from two independent members, and escalate to vendor support if two captains persist beyond documented convergence timers.

Case 2 — Captain auto-redirect storm after load balancer or DNS change: roll back recent GSLB edits, confirm peers resolve each other with stable addresses, and validate that management URLs used by operators are not accidentally steering heartbeat traffic.

Case 3 — KV store oplog lag from cold replica catch-up: verify maintenance windows, throttle heavy collection writes during catch-up, validate disk throughput on the trailing member, and postpone non-urgent bulk imports until lag decays.

Case 4 — Deployer push failure due to bundle path collision: remove duplicate app folders from the staging tree, rerun apply_shcluster-bundle with clean staging, and confirm checksum lines match across members before declaring victory.

Case 5 — Member rolled with mismatched build: identify the outlier with build_version join output, reinstall the correct package train, rebootstrap the member under change control, and watch conf replication return to steady state.

Case 6 — Certificate rotation breaking peer mutual TLS: reissue certs with overlapping trust, reload splunkd on members in the vendor-approved order, and validate cipher suites with openssl s_client probes from each peer subnet.

Case 7 — NTP drift causing RAFT timeouts: synchronize clocks on all members and the deployer, confirm stratum and leap-second handling, and replay consensus logs to verify election storms cease after skew correction.

Case 8 — Large saved-search migration backlog: pause non-essential scheduled work, redistribute acceleration objects, and add temporary search capacity before forcing another captain failover during the backlog drain.

Case 9 — OOM killer culling the captain: increase host memory or reduce per-process limits, identify heavy searches with Job Inspector, and relocate badly behaved scheduled reports before re-enabling aggressive alerting.

Case 10 — Garbage collection pause due to large dispatch directory: clean stale artifacts under vendor guidance, validate disk housekeeping cron or systemd timers, and expand fast storage for dispatch if sustained concurrency demands it.

Case 11 — ReplicationManager queue full during artifact storms: reduce concurrent bundle pushes, widen maintenance windows between app installs, and validate peer disk space for replication spool paths.

Case 12 — Dynamic captain rebuild during disk pressure: relieve disk fullness on the affected member, relocate hot volumes if architecture allows, and verify introspection CPU and I/O wait return to normal before closing the incident.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists _time, cluster_id, env, owner_team, captain_id, captain_changes_5m, member_count, member_offline_count, conf_replication_backlog, kv_store_oplog_lag_ms, deployer_push_status, member_p95_cpu_pct, severity, and recommended_action for analyst drilldowns.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the small council that runs interactive search like judges sharing a single gavel: when the chair changes too often, the shared binder stops matching, or the backstage clerk falls behind, we call the crew before the room hears silence.
hardware: Applies to Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud dedicated stacks running search head clusters on Linux peers or supported platforms where splunkd emits metrics.log and splunkd component logs to index=_internal, including virtual machines and containerized deployments with equivalent introspection visibility.
